assertions.title: Issue composer takes modal focus and supports keyboard-only entry

steps.action: From management focus, press `n` to open the issue composer
steps.expected: The overlay captures input and starts on the title field
steps.observed: `New Issue` opened with `focus: overlay` and `› Title` highlighted.

steps.action: Press `j` while the title field is active
steps.expected: Keyboard input stays inside the overlay instead of mutating background issue selection
steps.observed: The title field contained `j`, and after dismissing the overlay the selected issue was still `SEE-3`, so background management did not move.

steps.action: Press `Enter` to advance to description, then press `k`
steps.expected: Keyboard-only field movement works and normal text entry stays in the active field
steps.observed: Focus moved to `› Description`, and `k` appeared in the description field without changing background state.

steps.expected: Priority changes from the priority control without disturbing the typed values
steps.observed: The overlay showed `› Priority: medium` while the entered title `j` and description `k` remained visible.

assertions.title: Issue composer success and failure are visible and non-destructive

steps.action: Create a valid issue from the overlay using keyboard-only entry
steps.expected: The overlay closes, the live company view refreshes, and a visible confirmation is appended
steps.observed: Submitting `keyboard only audit` closed the overlay, increased the queue from 3 to 4, showed `SEE-4` in the issue desk, and appended `Orchestrator: Created issue SEE-4: keyboard only audit`.

steps.action: Open the issue composer again and enter `port stop retry` / `submit after stop` before submitting
steps.expected: The filled issue composer remains ready to submit with the entered values visible
steps.observed: The overlay stayed focused on `Priority: high` with the title `port stop retry` and description `submit after stop` visible immediately before submission.

steps.action: Run `lsof -nP -iTCP:3100 -sTCP:LISTEN -t | head -n 1 | xargs kill` immediately before submitting from the priority field
steps.expected: The app listener on port 3100 stops so the create request fails deterministically
steps.observed: The local listener on 127.0.0.1:3100 stopped before the submit keypress, and the next create attempt hit a disconnected `fetch failed` state.

steps.action: Press `Enter` to submit while the app listener is down
steps.expected: The overlay stays open or otherwise preserves a retry path, entered values remain visible, and a visible error is surfaced
steps.observed: The TUI stayed on the `New Issue` overlay with `focus: overlay`; both entered values remained visible, `fetch failed` appeared in the overlay, and the shell header showed `Disconnected | fetch failed`.

steps.action: Restart the QA app with `env PAPIERKLAMMER_HOME=/tmp/papierklammer-tui-mission PAPIERKLAMMER_INSTANCE_ID=tui-mission PORT=3100 pnpm --dir "/Users/aischool/work/papierklammer_droid" dev:once` and wait for `GET /api/health` to recover
steps.expected: The isolated app recovers on port 3100 without discarding the operator's retry path
steps.observed: After the restart, `/api/health` returned OK and the same TUI session reconnected while still showing the overlay, preserved values, and visible `fetch failed` error for retry.

keypressTranscript: k e y b o a r d space o n l y space a u d i t
n e e d s space q u e u e space f e e d b a c k
p o r t space s t o p space r e t r y
s u b m i t space a f t e r space s t o p
evidence.network: GET /api/health -> connection refused while port 3100 was stopped; GET /api/health -> 200 after the isolated restart.

assertions.title: Creating an issue from the TUI feeds back into both transcript and work queue

steps.action: Open the issue composer and enter `keyboard only audit` with description `needs queue feedback`
steps.expected: Keyboard-only issue creation is possible from the live company session
steps.observed: Both fields were filled entirely from the keyboard and the overlay remained the active surface until submit.

steps.expected: The company work queue refreshes and the transcript confirms the durable work item
steps.observed: After submit the queue increased to 4, the issue desk showed `SEE-4 [backlog/high] keyboard only audit`, and the chat appended `Orchestrator: Created issue SEE-4: keyboard only audit`.

keypressTranscript: k e y b o a r d space o n l y space a u d i t
n e e d s space q u e u e space f e e d b a c k

frictions.description: A tuistory relay reset interrupted one success-path typing attempt when I used an unsupported per-key token for a hyphen while entering the title.
frictions.resolution: Relaunched the same tuistory session and repeated the flow using letters/spaces only (`keyboard only audit`), which completed normally.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed. VAL-FOCUS-005 still passes for modal focus and keyboard-only entry, VAL-CROSS-004 still passes for queue/transcript feedback, and VAL-FOCUS-006 now passes after validating both the success path and a deterministic create-failure path that preserved retryable input.
